svg.text: Complete Request-Response Flow with Models, Views, URLs, and Database
• Headers: Cookie, CSRF Token, Content-Type | • Body: Form data (POST only)
Each middleware processes the request in order (top to bottom)
Purpose: Protect against common web attacks
✓ Checks CSRF token on POST requests
Example: Malicious site can't submit forms
to your site on behalf of logged-in user
✓ Sets AnonymousUser if not logged in
Request processed, now route to view
Matches request path to view function
Remaining path: live-monitor/ → Matches → Call views.live_monitor(request)
# Get cameras based on user role
Function receives: request object (with user, session, etc.)
View function processes request and prepares response
return redirect('login') # Redirect to login page
SQL: SELECT * FROM cameras_camera WHERE is_active = 1
SQL: SELECT * FROM cameras_camera WHERE id = 1 LIMIT 1
Django Models define database structure and relationships
| id | user_id | user_type | bio | phone | date_of_birth | display_name | avatar_url |
| id | name | rtsp_url | ip_address | port | username | password | stream_path | is_active |
User ←→ UserProfile (One user has one profile)
View renders template and returns HTTP response
1. HTTP Request arrives from client (browser) with path, headers, cookies
2. Middleware Stack processes request (security, session, CSRF, authentication)
3. URL Router matches path to view function (school_project/urls.py → cameras/urls.py)
4. View Function executes business logic, checks permissions, queries database via ORM
5. Django ORM translates Python code to SQL, queries database, returns model objects
6. Template Rendering combines HTML template with context data, generates HTTP response
